content.problemStatement:
You are given a graph with NNN vertices (numbered 111 to NNN) and MMM bidirectional edges, which doesn't contain multiple edges or self-loops — that is, the given graph is a simple undirected graph.

For each pair of vertices a,ba, ba,b such that 1≤a,b≤N1 \leq a, b \leq N1≤a,b≤N, it is possible to add a new edge between vertices aaa and bbb to the graph, with a cost of (a−b)2(a - b)^2(a−b)2.

Find the minimum cost of adding edges so that vertex NNN is reachable from vertex 111.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains 222 space-separated integers, NNN and MMM.
The ithi^{th}ith of the next MMM lines contains 222 space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting a bidirectional edge between vertex uiu_iui​ and vertex viv_ivi​ in the graph.


Output Format
For each test case, output the answer in a single line.
constraints: The graph doesn't contain self-loops or multiple edges
Sum of NNN over all testcases is no more than 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
Sum of MMM over all testcases is no more than 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: Add an edge between vertices 111 and 222, with cost (1−2)2=1(1-2)^2 = 1(1−2)2=1.
Test Case 222: Vertices 111 and 555 are already connected, so no edges need to be added.
